festivals.name: Nyepi (Day of Silence)
festivals.description: Bali's Hindu New Year is observed as a total day of silence — no lights, no work, no travel for 24 hours on the island.

festivals.name: Waisak at Borobudur
festivals.description: Thousands of Buddhist monks walk in procession from Mendut Temple to Borobudur as lanterns rise into the night sky.

festivals.name: Ubud Writers & Readers Festival
festivals.description: Asia's premier literary festival in Bali's cultural heart brings together international authors, ideas, and culture.

destinations.name: Bali
destinations.description: The Island of the Gods has sacred rice terraces, ornate temples, world-class surf, and a vibrant wellness culture.

destinations.name: Borobudur
destinations.description: The world's largest Buddhist monument, an 8th-century stupa complex with 2,672 relief panels and 504 Buddha statues.

destinations.name: Yogyakarta
destinations.description: Java's cultural capital has a royal palace, batik workshops, Prambanan's Hindu temples, and Mt Merapi volcano.

destinations.name: Raja Ampat
destinations.description: The epicentre of global marine biodiversity with over 600 coral species — widely considered the world's best diving.

destinations.name: Komodo National Park
destinations.description: The only place to see Komodo dragons in the wild, surrounded by world-class diving in pink-sand-beach islands.

destinations.name: Lombok & Mount Rinjani
destinations.description: A volcanic island with a 3,726m active volcano trek and the Gili Islands' turquoise waters and sea turtles.

destinations.name: Tana Toraja
destinations.description: A highland culture where elaborate funeral ceremonies last weeks and the dead are buried in cliff-face tombs with guardian effigies.

destinations.name: Bromo-Tengger-Semeru NP
destinations.description: Mount Bromo smokes above an alien sand-sea caldera, most dramatically seen at sunrise from the crater rim.
